summary: The novel follows a north-to-south overland journey from the Hoarded capital (the Vault) through progressively more depleted territory — Spent Towns, Thessa's settlement, the Margins — to the Scour, a vast erosion basin where the threshold to the sub-Ledger opens. Macro geography is load-bearing: the route tracks a social gradient from Hoarded wealth to systemic depletion, and travel time between zones constrains the pursuit plot. Micro geography is load-bearing only at the Scour (Ch 17–18), where tactical positioning across the basin determines the climax.

micro.structure_type: natural erosion basin with collapsed mining infrastructure

floor_summary.ridgeline: Elevated rim surrounding the basin. Exposed rock, wind-scoured. Sable's observation and approach point (Ch 17 intercut). Three hours' travel from the basin floor at cautious pace. Provides clear sightlines to the basin floor and Reserve positions below.
floor_summary.navigable_descent: The basin's primary entry route, a switchback path carved into the basin wall. Controlled by the Red Reserve garrison — two agents posted at the descent, the remaining two mobile on the basin floor. The only conventional way down.
floor_summary.collapsed_mining_tunnel: Alternate route bypassing the guarded descent. Old Shaping-ability mining left structural damage; the tunnel is partially collapsed and requires ability expenditure to traverse safely (light, reinforcement). Exits onto the basin floor east of the Reserve's primary sight lines. The garrison knows about it but cannot fully cover both the descent and the tunnel exit simultaneously.
floor_summary.basin_floor: Open ground of wind-eroded rock and exposed geological layers. Approximately two kilometers across. Minimal cover. Once on the floor, movement is visible from the ridgeline and from the Reserve's positions near the threshold point. The distance from the tunnel exit to the threshold point is roughly 800 meters of exposed terrain.
floor_summary.threshold_point: A specific location on the basin floor where the cascade mechanism activates and the threshold opens. Marked by no visible feature — Vel identifies it through Maren's route journal annotations and her own Perception mapping. The Reserve commander has guarded this exact spot for five years.

locations.name: Ridgeline
locations.description: Basin rim with sightlines to all positions below. Sable's approach vector.

locations.name: Navigable descent
locations.description: Switchback path down the basin wall. Reserve-controlled chokepoint.

locations.name: Collapsed mining tunnel
locations.description: Alternate route through old Shaping-mining infrastructure. Partially collapsed, requires ability expenditure to traverse. Exits east of the Reserve's main sight lines.

locations.name: Basin floor
locations.description: Open, wind-scoured rock. Approximately 2 km across, minimal cover. Exposed to observation from ridgeline and Reserve positions.

locations.name: Threshold point
locations.description: Specific unmarked location on the basin floor where the cascade activates. No visible marker. The Reserve commander's permanent post.

locations.name: Reserve garrison camp
locations.description: Permanent encampment near the threshold point. Two long-term operatives (including the five-year commander) plus two freshly deployed agents for the Kem retrieval. Positioned to cover the navigable descent and the threshold point, but stretched thin when also covering the tunnel exit.

key_routes.description: Open approach to the main basin entry. Visible, expected, Reserve-watched.
Lateral traverse along the rim to the tunnel mouth. Less visible than the descent approach.
Underground passage requiring ability expenditure (light, reinforcement). Exits east of Reserve sight lines. Kem spends 5 levels, Vel spends 4 levels in transit.
Approximately 800 meters of open ground. No cover. The Reserve detects them upon emergence. The commander calls out from the threshold point.
Sable's route. Three hours behind the protagonists. She can see Reserve positions from the rim and chooses her moment to descend.

locations.name: The Vault (Hoarded Capital)
locations.description: Northern seat of Hoarded power. Walled compound housing the aristocratic families. Interior includes the Conservatory hall (ritual space), residential wings, a sealed wing housing the Red Reserve, and a ceremonial gate with House signet stands. Kem departs in Ch 2; Sable departs in Ch 6. Characters do not return.

locations.name: Northern settlement
locations.description: Small farming settlement abandoned by the Hoarded to disease. Where Vel heals the injured farmer in Ch 1. North of the Vault — Vel heads north from here toward Kem.

locations.name: Forested highlands
locations.description: Wooded high ground south of the Vault. Site of the first outrider fight (Ch 4) and the camping/teaching sequence (Ch 5). Two days' travel south of the outrider encounter.

locations.name: Spent Towns
locations.description: Cluster of self-governing settlements where residents average level 150–200. Functional communities where people spend abilities on farming, building, and healing. Kem's first exposure to working people. Where Dren is hired (Ch 7). South of the forested highlands.

locations.name: Thessa's settlement
locations.description: Small settlement south of the Spent Towns where Thessa (level 78) has built a careful, low-spend life. A Hoarded economic representative operates here. Site of the confrontation over Vel's past and the escape from Hoarded agents (Ch 10).

locations.name: River crossing
locations.description: Between Thessa's settlement and the Margins. Bridge is out. Site of Kem's restraint-vs-ideology lesson (Ch 11). Genuinely dangerous — high water, slippery rocks.

locations.name: The Margins
locations.description: Extensive depleted territory where generations have been ground down by Hoarded exploitation. Depleted farmland, crumbling structures, indentured-labor infrastructure. Contains scattered settlements, a still-operating Hoarded labor operation, and the community where Fallow lives. Three days north of the Scour. Chapters 12–16 traverse this region.

locations.name: The Scour
locations.description: Vast wind-eroded basin at the southern extreme. Exposed layered rock, hostile terrain. The threshold to the sub-Ledger opens here. Permanently garrisoned by the Red Reserve. See micro layout for tactical detail.

locations.name: The sub-Ledger
locations.description: Civilization beneath the threshold. No Ledger, no abilities. Hand-quarried stone architecture, obsidian-edge medicine, council governance with voluntary/involuntary zero tensions. Accessed only by reaching level zero at the threshold point during an active cascade. Not geographically below the Scour in a literal sense — the threshold is a transition, not a descent.

routes.description: Vel travels north to find Kem. Unspecified duration — she arrives within days of his departure.
Southward travel after Vel and Kem join. Outriders catch them here (Ch 4). Several days' journey.
Continued south through open country. Approximately one week of travel including the camping sequence (Ch 5).
South with Dren as guide. Several days. Sable's team catches them en route (Ch 8, south of the Spent Towns).
South, crossing the river where the bridge is out (Ch 11). The river marks the approximate boundary between settled Spent territory and the Margins.
Three days south through depleted territory (per Fallow's account in Ch 12). The land drops into the erosion basin.
Not a geographic route. Requires reaching level zero during an active co-spending cascade at the threshold point. The transition is instantaneous — present, then elsewhere.

cardinal_anchors.north: The Vault and Hoarded capital — the seat of preservation, wealth, and political power.
cardinal_anchors.south: The Scour and the threshold — the journey's destination, the point of maximum depletion.
cardinal_anchors.gradient: The north-to-south axis tracks a social and economic gradient: Hoarded wealth → Spent Towns (functional spending) → Thessa's settlement (careful survival) → the Margins (exploitative depletion) → the Scour (zero). Each zone is more depleted than the last. The pursuit moves in the same direction, compressing distance as the novel progresses.
